tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P2] Strip leaked tracker strings from the 8 generic stage skills
tasks.description: Make the generic stage skills speak only the WorkItem model + capability verbs (charter G8). Audit-confirmed leak counts: triaging-work 16, verifying-work 10, capturing-work 10, closing-work 9, specifying-work 8, decomposing-work 6, tending-work 5 (and any in ideating-work). For each `.agents/flow/skills/<stage>/SKILL.md` (and its references/*), replace every Linear / Composio / DOR-specific reference with the generic equivalent: 'the linear-adapter skill' -> 'the adapter skill', 'Linear MCP' / 'composio execute LINEAR_*' -> 'the adapter's <verb> capability', 'the DOR team' / 'DOR-NNN' examples -> generic '<TRACKER>-NNN' placeholders, label family names stay generic (agent/ ready, stage/*). The ONLY skill permitted to name a tracker is linear-adapter (the adapter). Do NOT change behavior or the verb contract; this is a prose-only decoupling. Acceptance: a grep of the 8 stage skill dirs for /mcp__(plugin_)?linear/, /\bcomposio\b/i, /\bLINEAR_[A-Z_]+\b/, and bare /\bDOR-?\b/ team references returns zero hits outside linear-adapter. Render any work-item example as the generic identifier-with-title convention. This task is independent of Phase 1 and Phase 3 (parallelWith).
tasks.activeForm: Decoupling the stage skills from the tracker

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P2] Extend the tracker-confinement guard to assert the 8 stage skills are tracker-clean
tasks.description: Strengthen the tracker-confinement vitest guard so the G8 invariant is enforced, not just asserted by hand. The guard currently lives at packages/flow/src/__tests__/tracker-confinement.test.ts and walks FLOW_BUNDLE_ROOTS (skills, commands, packages/flow/src, .dork/tasks/flow-drain, the Stop hook). Add an explicit per-stage-skill assertion: each of the 8 generic stage skill dirs (everything under .agents/flow/skills/ EXCEPT linear-adapter and any adapters/ reference dir) must produce zero tracker offenders, and the scan over those dirs must be non-vacuous (each contributes >=1 scanned file). Keep the linear-adapter carve-out and the SCAN_EXCLUSIONS for the fixture/doc tests. If task 1.5 has already moved the guard to .agents/flow/engine/__tests__/ and updated repoRoot + roots, build on that home; otherwise leave the guard in place and only add the new assertion (1.5 re-homes it). Acceptance: the guard fails if any stage skill regains a tracker string, and the new per-skill assertion is present and green.
tasks.activeForm: Extending the tracker-confinement guard for G8

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P3] Author the adapter contract (SPEC.md + WorkItem schema + 13 verbs + invariants)
tasks.description: Write the tracker-adapter contract that any adopter's adapter must satisfy, at .agents/flow/adapters/SPEC.md. It defines: (1) the normalized WorkItem model (mirror packages/flow/src/work-item.ts: id, identifier, title, stateCategory in {backlog,unstarted,started,completed,canceled}, priority, type, ownershipClass, project, relations.blockedBy/blocks, labels, assignee, url, agentDisposition) and the rule that the engine matches on stateCategory NEVER on a tracker's display name; (2) the 13 capability verbs the engine calls (from the linear-adapter SKILL.md PMClient contract: getEligibleWork, getProjects, getProjectWork, resolveProject, transition, comment, createSubIssue, getRelations, assign, applyLabel, needsInput, search, plus the readiness/nudge verbs the existing adapter exposes — enumerate the exact set from .agents/flow/skills/linear-adapter/SKILL.md so the count is exactly 13). For each verb: signature (generic params + WorkItem-typed return), durability requirement, and graceful-degradation behavior; (3) the conformance invariants the validator checks: the five state categories present, required WorkItem fields non-null, blockedBy resolves to known ids, label re-namespacing (tracker-native labels -> generic agent/ stage/ families). Avoid em-dashes in the authored prose (use colons, parens, commas, or hyphens). Acceptance: SPEC.md enumerates exactly 13 verbs and the full WorkItem schema, and is internally consistent with linear-adapter SKILL.md. Independent of Phase 1 and Phase 2 (parallelWith).

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P3] Write validate-adapter.mjs conformance harness + fake in-memory tracker fixture
tasks.description: Author .agents/flow/scripts/validate-adapter.mjs: a dependency-free Node script that loads an adapter description (a fixture set of WorkItems the adapter would return, supplied as JSON on stdin or a --fixture path) and asserts every conformance invariant from SPEC.md (task 3.1): the five state categories are recognized, required WorkItem fields are present and well-typed, relations.blockedBy ids all resolve within the set, labels are in the re-namespaced generic families, and getEligibleWork only returns items carrying the agent/ ready label. JSON in, structured JSON verdict out ({ok:boolean, failures:[{invariant, detail}]}), exit 0 on pass / non-zero on fail, diagnostics to stderr, --help. Ship a fake in-memory tracker fixture under .agents/flow/adapters/reference/fixtures/ (a small WorkItem set with a blockedBy edge, all five categories, mixed labels) that the harness and the unit test both use; no live tracker. Add a vitest contract test (in the engine test home) that runs the harness on a known-good fixture (passes) and a known-bad fixture (fails loudly). Acceptance: validate-adapter.mjs is green on the good fixture and red with a precise failure on the bad one.

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P3] Write the building-adapters skill
tasks.description: Author .agents/flow/skills/building-adapters/SKILL.md: the guided procedure an adopter (or /flow:init) follows to produce a concrete adapter from the contract. Steps: (1) read SPEC.md and pick the closest reference adapter (Linear-MCP, Linear-Composio, or 'from scratch' for Jira/GitHub Issues/other); (2) map the adopter's tracker concepts to the WorkItem model + the 13 verbs (state -> stateCategory category mapping table, native labels -> generic families); (3) generate the adapter SKILL.md into the adopter's repo (.agents/flow/skills/<tracker>-adapter/ or the repo's skill home); (4) run node scripts/validate-adapter.mjs until green (generate-and-verify loop, re-prompt on each failed invariant). Keep the SKILL.md under ~500 lines; push verb-by-verb reference detail to references/. Avoid em-dashes. Acceptance: the skill's final step is the conformance loop and it references SPEC.md + the reference adapters by path.

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P3] Recast linear-adapter as the Linear-MCP and Linear-Composio reference adapters
tasks.description: Establish .agents/flow/adapters/reference/ with the two Linear reference adapters the contract ships. The existing .agents/flow/skills/linear-adapter/SKILL.md already implements the full PMClient contract over both Linear MCP and the Composio CLI; factor it into two named reference adapters: linear-mcp (the MCP-tool path) and linear-composio (the composio execute LINEAR_* path), each a SKILL.md that conforms to SPEC.md (task 3.1). These are the ONLY adapter files permitted to carry tracker strings (the tracker-confinement carve-out must cover .agents/flow/adapters/reference/ as well as the linear-adapter skill dir — coordinate the guard's ADAPTER carve-out in task 2.2/1.5). Keep the live linear-adapter skill as DorkOS's own in-repo adapter (it is this repo's concrete adapter); the reference copies are the templates adopters fork. Acceptance: both reference adapters pass node scripts/validate-adapter.mjs against the Linear fixture, and the tracker-confinement guard's carve-out covers the reference adapter dir.

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P1] Move the @dorkos/flow source + vitest suite into .agents/flow/engine/
tasks.description: Relocate the whole packages/flow/src/ tree (20 source files + the 23-file __tests__ suite, the 388 tests) into .agents/flow/engine/ as the canonical authoring home. The pure decision oracles (dispatch.ts, calibration.ts, gates.ts, flow-run.ts recovery, dispatch-outcome, the dedup in events.ts, config-schema.ts) become the source the CLI scripts wrap; the server-engine TS (transport.ts, reconcilers.ts, scheduler.ts, reconciler.ts, flow-state.ts, comms.ts, comment-response.ts, identity.ts, evidence.ts, work-item.ts, events.ts, index.ts) stays as TS source for the future P5 server to vendor or shell-out to (NOT deleted: that is DOR-88/90/95's consumer, and the no-dead-code rule applies — it has live tests). Preserve git history where practical (git mv). Add .agents/flow/engine/tsconfig.json + vitest.config.ts so the suite still runs in its new home. Do NOT yet delete packages/flow (task 1.5 does that after the build + rewire land) and do NOT yet change the tracker-confinement guard roots (1.5). Acceptance: the 388 tests pass from .agents/flow/engine/ via `pnpm vitest run .agents/flow/engine`. Independent of Phase 2 and Phase 3 (parallelWith); this is the head of the Phase 1 critical path.

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P1] Add the esbuild step that compiles oracle entrypoints to dependency-free .agents/flow/scripts/*.mjs
tasks.description: Because `dorkos package build` (DOR-145) does not exist yet, the interim build is esbuild (already a repo dev-dep; the CLI bundles with it). Add .agents/flow/engine/build.mjs (or a package script) that runs esbuild on each oracle CLI entrypoint (task 1.3) with --bundle --format=esm --platform=node --outdir=../scripts, emitting one self-contained .mjs per oracle. The pure oracles bundle to zero-runtime-dep .mjs (their imports are all `import type`, erased). The single Zod touch (validate-config.mjs, wrapping config-schema.ts) bundles Zod IN via esbuild, resolving the open question 'the config validator needs a runtime with dep resolution' without bun/deno. Wire the build into turbo.json + the engine's package scripts so `pnpm build` emits the scripts; commit the emitted .mjs (they are the shipped plugin artifact, like other committed dist in the repo) OR gitignore + build-on-CI per the repo's existing dist convention — match whichever the repo already uses for shipped bundles. Acceptance: `node .agents/flow/scripts/dispatch.mjs --help` works and the emitted .mjs import nothing at runtime except (for validate-config) the bundled Zod.

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P1] Author the oracle CLI wrappers + their CLI-contract tests
tasks.description: Write thin CLI entrypoints under .agents/flow/engine/cli/ that the build (1.2) compiles to scripts: dispatch.mjs (selectDispatch/classifyDispatchOutcome over WorkItem[] -> ranked + outcome), involvement.mjs (resolveInvolvement calibration ladder), gates.mjs (planApprovalRequired/tripsCircuitBreaker/evaluateAutoMerge), recovery.mjs (recoverOrphan), and validate-config.mjs (config-schema parse/validate). Each follows the agentskills.io script contract: read JSON from stdin (or a --input path), write JSON to stdout, --help with the input/output shape, meaningful exit codes (0 ok, 1 invalid input, 2 violated invariant), diagnostics to stderr, no interactive prompts. Add a vitest CLI-contract test per script (spawn the built .mjs with fixture JSON, assert stdout JSON + exit code) proving the deterministic logic is unchanged by the package->scripts move. Acceptance: every script has a --help and a green CLI-contract test; the dispatch script reproduces selectDispatch's ranking on a fixture identical to an existing unit test.

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P1] Rewire the stage skills + orchestrator + /flow auto to call the oracle scripts
tasks.description: Replace prose re-derivation of the ranking / calibration / gating / recovery ladders in the flow surfaces with calls to `node scripts/<oracle>.mjs` (JSON in, JSON out). Targets: the /flow orchestrator command (dispatch ranking), /flow auto (dispatch + outcome classification), the stage skills that today re-describe the calibration ladder (every stage references resolveInvolvement) and the gates (decomposing-work plan-approval, verifying-work auto-merge, the recovery ladder). The skill feeds the oracle the WorkItem[] the adapter produced and acts on the returned decision rather than re-deriving it in English. This eliminates the G1/G15 drift (one source of truth). MUST run AFTER task 2.1 (both edit the same stage skill files; sequencing avoids a double-edit conflict). Keep the prose that explains intent; remove only the re-derived algorithm, replacing it with the script call + how to interpret the result. Acceptance: no stage skill re-derives the 7-tier ranking or the calibration table inline; each instead invokes the named script, and a grep for the old ladder prose markers is clean.
tasks.activeForm: Rewiring the skills to call the oracle scripts

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P1] Delete packages/flow and purge every reference
tasks.description: Remove the packages/flow workspace package now that its source lives in .agents/flow/engine/ and the oracles ship as scripts. Delete packages/flow/ (package.json, tsconfig, eslint, vitest config, README, the src tree already moved). Purge references: pnpm-workspace.yaml (the packages/flow glob is covered by packages/*, so confirm nothing else points at @dorkos/flow), turbo.json (any flow-specific pipeline entry), any tsconfig path mapping, contributing/ docs that name @dorkos/flow, and the README's package list. Re-home the tracker-confinement guard: move it to .agents/flow/engine/__tests__/, update repoRoot depth, swap the packages/flow/src root for .agents/flow/engine, and extend the ADAPTER carve-out to cover .agents/flow/adapters/reference/ (task 3.4). Verify `grep -rn '@dorkos/flow'` across the repo returns only intentional historical references (ADR/spec prose) — no live import or workspace dep. Acceptance: `pnpm install` + `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm test` are green with no @dorkos/flow package present and the guard passing from its new home. Depends on 1.1 (source moved) and 1.4 (skills no longer conceptually depend on the package).

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P1] Confirm the 388 tests + new CLI-contract tests run in the engine home and are wired into the runner
tasks.description: Ensure the moved vitest suite (388 tests) and the new CLI-contract + adapter-conformance tests are discovered by the repo test runner from .agents/flow/engine/. Add the engine vitest project to the root test wiring (turbo `test` task + any workspace vitest config) so `pnpm test -- --run` includes them, since .agents/flow is not a workspace package. Confirm DEV-env-sensitive tests still behave (use `pnpm test -- --run`, not bare `pnpm vitest run`, per the known DEV-env gotcha). Acceptance: `pnpm test -- --run` includes and passes the engine suite + the new script tests; no test is orphaned by the move. Depends on 1.1 (moved) and 1.3 (new script tests exist).
tasks.activeForm: Wiring the engine tests into the runner

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P4] Define the repo-local config shape: committed config + gitignored secrets + env override
tasks.description: Specify and scaffold the per-adopter config the plugin reads: .agents/flow/config.json (committed: tracker name, identity mode, project routing, autonomy + gate defaults, the WIP caps — the existing FlowConfigSchema shape) plus a gitignored .agents/flow/config.local.json (secrets: tracker tokens, the Composio account handle) plus env-var override precedence (env > .local > config.json > schema defaults, mirroring the CLI's documented precedence). The authoritative schema stays the engine's config-schema.ts; ship config.schema.json (the JSON Schema bridge, already produced by generate-config-schema) next to config.json for editor validation. Document the precedence + the .gitignore entry. Acceptance: a documented config triad with a JSON-Schema-validated config.json, a gitignored .local for secrets, and a stated env-override order; validate-config.mjs (task 1.3) accepts a sample config.json.

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P4] Write the /flow:init first-run setup (command + skill)
tasks.description: Author .claude/commands/flow/init.md (thin trigger) over a new .agents/flow/skills/initializing-flow/ skill that runs first-run setup: (1) detect no config -> start; (2) prompt (via the calibration ladder's AskUserQuestion in interactive mode, or sane defaults headless) for tracker + connection (Linear-MCP / Linear-Composio / Jira / GitHub Issues / other), identity mode, and project routing; (3) invoke building-adapters (task 3.3) to generate the concrete adapter and run validate-adapter.mjs until green; (4) write .agents/flow/config.json (task 4.1), create the gitignored config.local.json, and add it to .gitignore; (5) confirm by running a dry `node scripts/dispatch.mjs` on an empty queue. Avoid em-dashes. Acceptance: /flow:init produces a committed config + gitignored secrets + a conformance-green generated adapter, idempotently (re-running detects existing config and offers to reconfigure rather than clobbering).

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P4] Route the orchestrator to /flow:init on missing config
tasks.description: Wire the /flow orchestrator command so that, on invocation, it detects an absent or invalid .agents/flow/config.json and routes to /flow:init before any stage work (the first-run UX in the spec's User Experience section). When config is present, behave exactly as today. Keep it a thin guard at the top of the orchestrator's routing. Acceptance: a fresh repo with no config, on `/flow`, lands in /flow:init; a configured repo is unaffected.
tasks.activeForm: Routing the orchestrator to first-run setup

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P5] Thin the flow-drain tick to delegate to the canonical single-tick
tasks.description: Reduce .dork/tasks/flow-drain/SKILL.md to a thin schedulable tick: keep the cron + enabled:false frontmatter (the opt-in safety gate per ADR-0295 bring-your-own-scheduler), and replace any inlined drain logic in its body with a delegation to the canonical single tick (recovery -> inbox/resume -> dispatch one item -> carry to its gate -> stop), i.e. one `/flow continue` tick, NOT `/flow auto` (no sentinel, no Stop-hook loop; the scheduler provides repetition). Note in the body that the final discovery home (a skill carrying cron+enabled, surfaced anywhere skills live) lands with DOR-150's capability model; this interim keeps the .dork/tasks/ home so autonomy is not blocked. Keep it tracker-clean (the confinement guard scans this dir). Acceptance: flow-drain is enabled:false, carries no drain algorithm of its own, and delegates to the single-tick; the confinement guard still passes on the dir.

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P7] Amend ADR-0281/0229, finalize draft ADRs, add the docs pages
tasks.description: Cross-cutting documentation: (1) amend ADR-0281 (the governing 'ship /flow as a plugin-type package' decision) to reflect the resolved repo home (canonical .agents/flow/, no separate repo) and the scripts-engine direction (DOR-134 carries this); (2) note the ADR-0229 amendment to the capability model (scheduling via cron+enabled, not a `kind` discriminator) (DOR-150 carries this); (3) keep the three new draft ADRs (0294 delete @dorkos/flow, 0295 bring-your-own-scheduler, 0296 generated adapters) consistent with what actually shipped, updating their status as the phases land; (4) add docs/guides/flow/ pages: 'install in your project', 'build your adapter' (points at SPEC.md + building-adapters), and 'turn on autonomy (bring-your-own-scheduler)'. Avoid em-dashes. Acceptance: the ADR set is internally consistent with the shipped code and the three docs pages exist. Independent of the code phases; can land last.
tasks.activeForm: Writing the cross-cutting docs and ADRs

tasks.subject: [flow-marketplace-package] [P6] Assemble + dogfood-install the flow plugin package (BLOCKED)
tasks.description: BLOCKED: requires DOR-145 (`dorkos package build`) and DOR-138 (the projection engine), which are not built yet. When unblocked: author .dork/manifest.json + .claude-plugin/plugin.json declaring the layer set (commands / skills / hooks / templates + scripts), have `dorkos package build` assemble the package from the canonical .agents/flow/ source, install it back into dorkos (dogfood), and run the install smoke test (build -> install into a temp repo -> /flow:init -> a dry tick). Do NOT attempt this phase in the current execution pass; leave it as the tracked follow-up. Acceptance (future): the package builds, installs into a clean repo, and a dry tick runs green. Depends on 1.5, 2.2, 3.4, 4.2 internally and on DOR-145 + DOR-138 externally.
